82	   `pushforwardComp` + the two new helpers. ~40 LOC. Underlying `adj : F ⊣ G` for the open
83	   immersion is `f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction` (`IsOpenMap.adjunction`,
84	   `TopCat/Opens.lean:307`, EXISTS); `H₁`/`H₂` are the `:346-353` proofs ported verbatim.
85	4. `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ := (pushforwardPushforwardAdj …).leftAdjointUniq
86	   (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ.hom)` (or `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq` applied to the two
87	   adjunctions). ~2 LOC.
88	
89	Total ~70-90 LOC. **No sub-step is itself a Mathlib-absent primitive** — unlike the iter-214 `d.1`
90	failure (which needed genuinely new R_x-linear stalk infrastructure), every ingredient here is
91	either an existing decl or a mechanical de-sheafification of an existing sheaf-level decl that
92	already operates on presheaf data.
93	
94	- **Verdict**: PROCEED (route correct) + NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (the three new decls, bounded).

96	### Decision 4: H2 presheaf monoidal lift — bounded / no gap?

104	- **Gap**: identical idiom available. **Verdict**: PROCEED (bounded, no gap), confirming the

107	## Recommendation
108	**H1 is buildable from current Mathlib — definitive YES.** Build the three presheaf decls above
109	(`pushforwardNatTrans`, `pushforwardCongr`, `pushforwardPushforwardAdj`) by de-sheafifying
110	`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean` (every line there already manipulates `.val` presheaf data), then
111	close with `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq` against the **existing** `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` — do

115	absent primitive. Commit the prover budget.

1	\chapter{Relative Picard sheaf --- \texttt{Scheme.Modules.tensorObj} substrate (A.1.c.SubT)}
2	\label{chap:Picard_TensorObjSubstrate}
3	
4	\section{Motivation: the abelian-group-valued relative Picard sheaf}
5	\label{sec:tensorobj_motivation}
6	
7	Fix a base field \(k\) and a smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C\) over
8	\(k\). In Kleiman's framework (\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor},
9	\cref{def:rel_pic_sharp}), the relative Picard functor
10	\[
11	  \Pic_{C/k} \;\;\colon\;\; (\Sch/k)^{op} \;\longrightarrow\; \AddCommGrpCat,
12	  \qquad
13	  T \;\longmapsto\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, \pi_T^* \Pic(T),
14	\]
15	is, by definition, abelian-group-valued. The abelian-group structure on each
16	fiber \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) comes from tensor product of line bundles
17	(Stacks tag 01CR / Hartshorne~II.\S 6), and the structure descends to the
18	quotient because \(\pi_T^*\) is a group homomorphism.
19	
20	% SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
21	% Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard functors are
22	% abelian-group-valued; the relative one is defined as a quotient of abelian
23	% groups)
24	% (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
25	% SOURCE QUOTE:
26	% "\begin{dfn}\label{df:aPf}
27	%  The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
28	%  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
29	% abelian groups defined by the formula
30	% 	$$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
31	% \end{dfn}
32	%  [\dots]
33	% \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
34	%   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
35	% 	$$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
36	%  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
37	% topologies  by
38	% 	$$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
39	% 	\Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
40	%  \end{dfn}"
41	\textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2, Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs.
42	The absolute and relative Picard functors take values in the category of
43	abelian groups; the relative functor is the quotient of one such group by
44	another, and the structure morphisms of the resulting presheaf are group
45	homomorphisms.}
46	
47	Concretely, the abelian-group law on \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the assignment
48	\(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr] :=
49	\bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
50	with neutral element the class of the structure sheaf
51	\(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\) and inverse
52	\(- \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\), where
53	\(\mathcal{L}^{-1} = \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}}(\mathcal{L},
54	\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T})\) is the dual invertible sheaf. Pullback of
55	\(\mathcal{O}\)-modules along \(\pi_T : C \times_k T \to T\) preserves tensor
56	products and the structure sheaf, so the image
57	\(\pi_T^* \Pic(T) \subset \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a subgroup, and the quotient
58	\(\Pic(C \times_k T) / \pi_T^* \Pic(T)\) inherits a canonical abelian-group
59	structure. This is the content of
60	\cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}.
61	
62	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
63	infrastructural, and --- crucially --- much smaller than a full
64	monoidal-category construction. The group law lives on \emph{isomorphism
65	classes} of line bundles: each group axiom is a \emph{proposition} asserting
66	the existence of an isomorphism (\(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\,\cdots \cong \cdots)\)),
67	and a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it. In
68	particular the coherence data of a monoidal category --- the pentagon,
69	triangle and hexagon identities, and a fortiori any closed structure --- is
70	\emph{never consumed} by a group law on iso-classes. What the law genuinely
71	requires on the carrier is therefore only:
72	\begin{enumerate}
73	  \item a binary tensor-product operation
74	    \(\otimes\, : \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \times \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
75	                  \to \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), well-defined on
76	    isomorphism classes;
77	  \item the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X\) as a designated unit object
78	    for \(\otimes\);
79	  \item the \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate
80	    (\(\exists N,\ M \otimes_X N \cong \mathcal{O}_X\)) together with the three
81	    monoidal coherence isomorphisms of \(\otimes_X\): associativity
82	    \((M \otimes N) \otimes P \cong M \otimes (N \otimes P)\), unit
83	    \(\mathcal{O}_X \otimes M \cong M\) (and on the right), and commutativity
84	    \(M \otimes N \cong N \otimes M\). The inverse is carried by the
85	    predicate, not by a separate lemma.
86	\end{enumerate}
87	At Mathlib's pinned commit one might be tempted to obtain (3) by instantiating a
88	\emph{full} symmetric monoidal category on \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) and
89	reading the three isomorphisms off it. Two Mathlib facts make such a route
90	conceivable. First, the category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) of presheaves
91	of modules over a presheaf of commutative rings \(R\) is already a (symmetric)
92	monoidal category in Mathlib (\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}\)),
93	with the sectionwise relative tensor \((M \otimes_R N)(U) = M(U) \otimes_{R(U)}
94	N(U)\); this applies verbatim to the \emph{varying} structure sheaf
95	\(R = \mathcal{O}_X\). Second, Mathlib's localization-of-monoidal-categories API
96	(\(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\)) takes a
97	monoidal category \(C\), a localization functor \(L : C \to D\) at a morphism
98	property \(W\), and an instance \(W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), and produces a
99	\emph{full} monoidal-category structure on the localization \(D\); sheafification
100	of presheaves of modules is the localization \(L = \mathtt{sheafification}\) at the
101	locally-bijective class \(W = J.W\), so a full monoidal structure on
102	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) would follow once \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) were
103	supplied.
104	
105	This full-monoidal-category route is, however, \emph{not} the goal, and is not
106	pursued. The goal is the commutative \emph{group} on locally-trivial iso-classes
107	(à la \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\)), and that group law consumes only propositions, so
108	it never needs a coherent monoidal category --- neither the
109	pentagon/triangle/hexagon identities nor the \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instance
110	that the \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) API requires. What it needs is exactly the
111	\emph{existence} of the three coherence isomorphisms of (3), and these are produced
112	directly: the associator by gluing canonical local isomorphisms
113	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, via the single linchpin
114	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), the unitors by the cheap \(\mathtt{mapIso}\)
115	pattern (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}), and the braiding likewise
116	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}). With those three existence facts the group law
117	assembles \emph{by hand} the commutative group of \(\otimes\)-invertible
118	\(\otimes\)-iso-classes --- the Picard group
119	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) --- each group-axiom field fed a single
120	existence-of-isomorphism. This is the scheme-theoretic analogue of Mathlib's
121	fixed-ring \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R := \mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\),
122	whose \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) is read off the \(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) of the coherent
123	monoidal category \(\mathtt{MonoidalCategory}(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) that
124	Mathlib supplies over a fixed ring; the present construction cannot take that
125	\(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) route, since the coherent monoidal category on
126	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) for the varying structure sheaf is exactly what it
127	avoids. The
128	\(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible},
129	Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\) idiom) carries each inverse existentially.
130	The whole \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) / \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) / stalk
131	apparatus is therefore vestigial for the group law and off the critical path.
132	
133	\section{Mathlib API survey}
134	\label{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}
135	
136	The relevant pieces of the Mathlib API at the pinned commit
137	(\texttt{b80f227}) were originally assembled into a single \emph{realization route}
138	(route~(e)): obtaining a full monoidal structure on the substrate by instantiating
139	Mathlib's abstract localization-of-monoidal-categories API rather than
140	hand-assembling the associator. That route is \emph{superseded}. The group law on
141	iso-classes never consumes a coherent monoidal category, and the associator is
142	obtained by direct gluing through \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, so the
143	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instance on which route~(e) hinges is never required.
144	The survey below is retained as a Mathlib-API reference and to record why the route
145	is vestigial; the four Mathlib facts are all verified against the on-disk Mathlib
146	source.
147	
148	\paragraph{(i) The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category.} Mathlib's
149	\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal} constructs a
150	monoidal-category instance
151	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}\) on
152	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,(R \circ \mathtt{forget}_2\,\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,
153	\mathtt{RingCat})\), with the sectionwise relative tensor
154	\[
155	  (M \otimes_R N)(U) \;\;=\;\; M(U) \otimes_{R(U)} N(U)
156	\]
157	% SOURCE: [Mathlib], `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct` /
158	% `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`, in
159	% `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean` (L104, L125)
160	% (read from .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean).
161	% SOURCE QUOTE:
162	% "noncomputable instance monoidalCategoryStruct :
163	%     MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
164	%   tensorObj := tensorObj
165	%   whiskerLeft _ _ _ g := tensorHom (𝟙 _) g
166	%   whiskerRight f _ := tensorHom f (𝟙 _)
167	%   ...
168	%   tensorUnit := unit _
169	%   associator M₁ M₂ M₃ := isoMk (fun _ ↦ α_ _ _ _) ...
170	% noncomputable instance monoidalCategory :
171	%     MonoidalCategory (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where ..."
172	\textit{Source: [Mathlib], \texttt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct} /
173	\texttt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}, in
174	\texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean}, L104--L125.}
175	together with associator, left/right unitors and braiding making it symmetric
176	monoidal. The base presheaf of rings \(R \circ \mathtt{forget}_2\,
177	\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,\mathtt{RingCat}\) is exactly the project's
178	\(\mathtt{Sheaf.val}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) carrier (equal by \(\mathtt{rfl}\)),
179	so this monoidal structure applies verbatim to the \emph{varying} structure sheaf
180	\(\mathcal{O}_X\) --- no project work. This is the presheaf-level analogue of
181	Stacks tag 03DM (relative tensor product of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules).

183	\paragraph{(ii) The localization-of-monoidal-categories API.} Mathlib's
184	\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Basic} provides the class
185	\(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\) and the construction
186	\(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\). The class records exactly
187	the whiskering-stability data:

189	% `CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal`, in

191	% (read from .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Localization/Monoidal/Basic.lean).
192	% SOURCE QUOTE:
193	% "class IsMonoidal : Prop extends W.IsMultiplicative where
194	%   whiskerLeft (X : C) {Y₁ Y₂ : C} (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂) (hg : W g) : W (X ◁ g)
195	%   whiskerRight {X₁ X₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (hf : W f) (Y : C) : W (f ▷ Y)
196	%  ...

199	%  ...

205	Given \([\MonoidalCategory\,C]\), \([W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}]\), a localization
206	functor \(L : C \to D\) with \([L.\mathtt{IsLocalization}\,W]\) and an isomorphism
207	\(\varepsilon : L(\mathbf{1}_C) \cong \mathtt{unit}\), the localized category
208	\(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\,L\,W\,\varepsilon\) is a \(\MonoidalCategory\) with
209	\emph{all} coherence (associator, unitors, pentagon, triangle, naturalities)

211	hypothesis chain.

213	\paragraph{(iii) The proof template for $W$-monoidality.} Mathlib's
214	\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW} proves
215	\((J.W\,(A := A)).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) \emph{stalkwise} for presheaves valued in a
216	\emph{fixed} monoidal concrete category \(A\) (i.e.\ \(C^{op} \to A\)), via the
217	conservative-family characterisation \(\mathtt{hP.W\_iff}\),
218	\(\mathtt{Functor.Monoidal.map\_tensor}\) and typeclass inference:
219	% SOURCE: [Mathlib], `CategoryTheory.ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.isMonoidal_W`
220	% and the `(J.W).IsMonoidal` instance, in
221	% `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean` (L48, L57)
222	% (read from .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean).
223	% SOURCE QUOTE:

225	%     [J.HasSheafCompose (forget A)] :
226	%     (J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal :=
227	%   .mk' _ (fun f g hf hg ↦ by
228	%     simp only [hP.W_iff (A := A)] at hf hg ⊢
229	%     intro Φ
230	%     rw [Functor.Monoidal.map_tensor]
231	%     infer_instance)"
232	\textit{Source: [Mathlib],
233	\texttt{ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.isMonoidal\_W}, in
234	\texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean}, L48--L57.}
235	This is the \emph{template} for the proof technique used in route~(e), but it does
236	\emph{not} apply directly to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\): modules over a
237	\emph{varying} ring are not of the form \(C^{op} \to A\) for a fixed monoidal
238	\(A\).
239	
240	\paragraph{The gap and route~(e), superseded.} The substrate
241	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\) of this chapter is the
242	sheafification of \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\), and
243	sheafification of presheaves of modules is exactly the localization
244	\(L = \mathtt{sheafification}\) at the locally-bijective class \(W = J.W\). Route~(e)
245	proposed to obtain a \emph{full} monoidal structure on
246	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\)
247	by applying \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) (ii) to the already-monoidal category (i),
248	which would require the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (its two
249	whiskering-stability fields \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}, with load-bearing
250	local-injectivity half \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}). This is
251	\emph{not} how the group law is realized. The group law consumes only the
252	\emph{existence} of the three coherence isomorphisms, and the associator is built
253	directly by gluing canonical local isomorphisms through
254	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}); it never invokes
255	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), a coherent monoidal category, or the stalkwise
256	whiskering apparatus. The route~(e) instance is therefore vestigial and off the
257	critical path.
258	
259	The stalk-infrastructure analysis that route~(e) turned on is recorded here for
260	reference only, since that route is superseded. The \emph{stalk module structure}
261	is already present in Mathlib: the file
262	\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk} supplies, for a topological
263	space \(X\), a presheaf of commutative rings \(R\) on \(X\) and a point
264	\(x \in X\), the \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-module structure on the stalk
265	\(\mathtt{stalk}\,M.\mathtt{presheaf}\,x\) of a presheaf of modules \(M\),
266	together with the germ--scalar compatibility \(\mathtt{germ\_smul}\); a linearity
267	packaging of the induced stalk map of a morphism was built project-side
268	(\cref{lem:stalk_linear_map}, the \emph{linearity half} of ingredient (d.1)).
269	Route~(e) additionally required a (d.1)-\emph{bridge} between the site-level
270	\(J.W\) and the topological stalkwise-isomorphism criterion on
271	\(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\), and (d.2) the commutation \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x
272	\otimes_{R_x} B_x\) of the stalk with the relative module-presheaf tensor; there is
273	moreover no monoidal \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\) in Mathlib. Because the group law
274	is realized through the direct-gluing associator (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso},
275	via \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}) and never through
276	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), none of this stalk apparatus is on the critical path;
277	it is retained only to document the superseded route.
278	
279	The project's policy on this gap is \emph{project-side}: the substrate is
280	built inside \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/} (the file
281	\(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean}\) is its home)
282	and the consumer instance is closed against it. A parallel Mathlib upstream
283	PR with the same content is welcome but not on the critical path for the
284	project; the consumer is closed against the project-side substrate
285	independently of any upstream timing.

287	\section{The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)}
288	\label{sec:tensorobj_substrate}
289	
290	\begin{definition}
291	
292	\leanok
293	[The substrate operation $\otimes$ on $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$]
294	  \label{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
295	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
296	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. For
297	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), define
298	  \[
299	    M \otimes_X N \;\;\in\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
300	  \]
301	  as follows. Choose an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}_{i \in I}\)
302	  of \(X\). On each \(U_i\), the restriction
303	  \(M|_{U_i}\) corresponds (via the equivalence between
304	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A_i}\)-modules and \(A_i\)-modules) to an
305	  \(A_i\)-module \(M_i\), and similarly
306	  \(N|_{U_i}\) corresponds to an \(A_i\)-module \(N_i\). Define
307	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\) as the sheafification of the presheaf
308	  \(U_i \mapsto M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i\). On overlaps
309	  \(U_i \cap U_j = \Spec A_{ij}\) the resulting modules agree via the
310	  canonical compatibility
311	  \((M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i) \otimes_{A_i} A_{ij}
312	     \;\cong\;
313	     (M_j \otimes_{A_j} N_j) \otimes_{A_j} A_{ij}\),
314	  so the data \(\{(M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\}\) glue to a single object
315	  \(M \otimes_X N\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The result is
316	  canonically independent of the affine cover chosen.
317	
318	  Equivalently: the underlying presheaf of \(M \otimes_X N\) is the
319	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product
320	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) of the underlying
321	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\), composed with the sheafification
322	  functor on the small Zariski site of \(X\). The substrate operation
323	  \(\otimes_X\) is, by construction, a \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
324	  object reflecting the relative tensor product of
325	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described in Stacks tag 03DM.
326	\end{definition}
327	
328	\begin{lemma}
329	
330	\leanok
331	[Functoriality of $\otimes_X$]
332	  \label{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
333	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality}
334	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
335	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The assignment
336	  \((M, N) \mapsto M \otimes_X N\) of
337	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} extends to a bifunctor
338	  \[
339	    \otimes_X \;\;\colon\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\times\;
340	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\;\longrightarrow\;\;
341	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
342	  \]
343	  natural in both arguments: a pair of morphisms
344	  \(f : M \to M'\) and \(g : N \to N'\) determines a morphism
345	  \(f \otimes g : M \otimes_X N \to M' \otimes_X N'\) compatible with
346	  identities and composition. This bifunctorial action on morphisms is
347	  the only datum the present lemma provides. The further coherence data
348	  of a monoidal structure --- the unitors
349	  \[
350	    \lambda_M \;\colon\; \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
351	    \qquad
352	    \rho_M \;\colon\; M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
353	  \]
354	  the associator
355	  \(\alpha_{M, N, P} : (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim}
356	   M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\), and the braiding
357	  \(\beta_{M, N} : M \otimes_X N \xrightarrow{\sim} N \otimes_X M\) ---
358	  are \emph{not} outputs of this lemma; they are off the critical path,
359	  recorded in \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
360	\end{lemma}
361	
362	\begin{proof}
363	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
364	  Pick an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}\) of \(X\). On each
365	  affine, the assignment is the standard tensor product of modules over a
366	  commutative ring \(A_i\), which is bifunctorial. The bifunctorial action
367	  on morphisms glues across the cover because the underlying gluing data of
368	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} respect the canonical morphisms on
369	  overlaps; equivalently, the morphism action is inherited from
370	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) (Mathlib module
371	  \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}) under
372	  sheafification. The unitor/associator/braiding coherence data are
373	  \emph{not} constructed here; they are off the critical path
374	  (\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}).
375	\end{proof}
376	
377	\begin{remark}
378	[The full monoidal category on all of $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$ is off-path; the group law consumes only propositions]
379	  \label{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}
380	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
381	        lem:whisker_of_W}
382	  One could ask for the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) of
383	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, together with
384	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), \(\alpha\), \(\lambda\), \(\rho\) and \(\beta\), to
385	  assemble into a full symmetric \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
386	  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), discharging the pentagon,
387	  triangle and hexagon coherence identities. This is explicitly \emph{off-path}
388	  and \emph{not pursued}. One could in principle obtain it as the output of
389	  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) applied to the
390	  already-monoidal presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)
391	  and the sheafification localizer \(J.W\), supplying the instance
392	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}); but the group law never
393	  consumes a coherent monoidal category, so this construction is superseded and not
394	  carried out. The substrate provides only the \emph{existence} of the three
395	  coherence isomorphisms that the group law actually consumes.
396	
397	  Were that superseded route pursued, the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\)
398	  would need \emph{no} closed structure: its
399	  \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) field \(W\,g \Rightarrow W(F \triangleleft g)\) for an
400	  \emph{arbitrary} module \(F\) is the flatness-free stalkwise statement
401	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} (a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a
402	  stalkwise isomorphism, so \((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes
403	  g_x\) is again an isomorphism for any \(F\)), the same technique Mathlib uses for
404	  fixed-base presheaves in
405	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\). It would use neither
406	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) nor the
407	  sectionwise-flatness hypothesis of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (which is
408	  false over a non-affine open and is likewise off the critical path). But this
409	  apparatus is not on the critical path at all: the group law is realized without
410	  it.
411	
412	  Independently of how the monoidal structure is built, the relative Picard
413	  group law of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is a structure on
414	  \emph{isomorphism classes} of line bundles, and every group axiom there is a
415	  \emph{proposition} of the form \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\).
416	  Such a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it, so the
417	  coherence data of the monoidal category --- pentagon, triangle, hexagon, and
418	  a fortiori any monoidal-closed structure --- is never \emph{consumed} by the
419	  group law. The group law needs only the existence of the three coherence
420	  isomorphisms \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,%
421	  lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, supplied directly: the associator by gluing canonical
422	  local isomorphisms through the restriction-compatibility isomorphism
423	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), and the
424	  unitors and braiding by the cheap \(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern
425	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}). The carrier --- iso-classes
426	  of the substrate tensor \(\otimes_X\) --- is the scheme-theoretic analogue of
427	  Mathlib's Picard group \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R := \mathrm{Skeleton}(
428	  \mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}), but it
429	  is built \emph{differently}: Mathlib reads its \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) off the
430	  \(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) of the coherent monoidal category
431	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalCategory}(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) over a fixed ring,
432	  whereas here --- with no coherent monoidal category on
433	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) available for the varying structure sheaf --- the
434	  group is assembled by hand from the existence-of-isomorphism facts.
435	\end{remark}
436	
437	\section{The lift through \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)}
438	\label{sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
439	
440	The consumer in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} works not with
441	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\) directly but with the subtype
442	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) of locally-trivial
443	modules on the fiber product \(C \times_k T\), introduced in
444	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}. This section records the
445	\emph{existence-of-isomorphism facts on line bundles} (associativity, unit,
446	commutativity and inverse) that the relative Picard group law consumes. These facts
447	are supplied \emph{directly}: associativity by the direct-gluing associator
448	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (through the linchpin
449	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), unit and commutativity by the cheap
450	\(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}),
451	and the inverse existentially from \(\otimes\)-invertibility. They are \emph{not}
452	read off any \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure: the route that would derive them from
453	\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal} (route~(e)) is superseded and off-path. The section also
454	records the closure of \(\otimes\) on the locally-trivial
455	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\) subtype (\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}) and
456	the interaction with the pullback functor \(\pi_T^*\). The open-immersion
457	restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is on the
458	critical path precisely because it is consumed by the direct-gluing associator
459	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}; it is \emph{not} tied to the superseded
460	whiskering-stability lemma \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, which belongs
461	to the off-path route~(e).
462	
463	\begin{lemma}
464	
465	\leanok
466	[Sectionwise lax-monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\)]
467	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal}
468	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal}
469	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
470	  % NOTE: this CommRingCat-level lax-monoidal instance
471	  % (`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`, with helpers
472	  % `restrictScalarsLaxε`, `restrictScalarsLaxμ`) is a self-contained
473	  % supplement. It is NOT used by `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, whose proof
474	  % (open-immersion sectionwise base change) identifies restriction along an
475	  % open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
476	  % needs no lax-monoidal pushforward. Retained for potential reuse; off the
477	  % critical path for the tensor group law.
478	  Let \(\varphi : R \to S\) be a morphism of presheaves of \emph{commutative}
479	  rings on a site (so that both \(R\) and \(S\) factor through
480	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) and the category of presheaves of modules over each
481	  is monoidal). Then the presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor
482	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) is lax monoidal.
483	  Consequently, composing with the already-monoidal Mathlib functor
484	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) via
485	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\), the full pushforward
486	  \[
487	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi
488	      \;\;:=\;\;
489	    \mathtt{pushforward}_0\,F\,R \;\circ\; \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi
490	  \]
491	  is lax monoidal. This lemma is a self-contained
492	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-level lax-monoidal supplement: it stands on its own
493	  and is retained for potential reuse, but it is \emph{off the critical path}
494	  for the tensor group law. In particular it is \emph{not} an ingredient of
495	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, whose proof identifies restriction along an
496	  open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
497	  consequently needs no lax-monoidal structure on the pushforward.
498	\end{lemma}
499	
500	\begin{proof}
501	  This is a \emph{sectionwise} lift of the existing fact that, for a ring map
502	  \(f : R \to S\), the module restriction-of-scalars functor
503	  \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,f\) is lax monoidal
504	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction}, where it is
505	  obtained as the \(\mathtt{rightAdjointLaxMonoidal}\) of the
506	  extension/restriction adjunction \(\mathtt{extendRestrictScalarsAdj}\) ---
507	  i.e.\ Mathlib already uses the same mate idiom one level down). The presheaf
508	  monoidal structure is computed sectionwise,
509	  \((M_1 \otimes M_2).\mathrm{obj}\,X = M_1.\mathrm{obj}\,X \otimes M_2.\mathrm{obj}\,X\),
510	  and the presheaf functor \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) acts
511	  sectionwise as \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{app}\,X)\).
512	  Hence the lax structure maps \(\mu\) (laxity) and \(\varepsilon\) (unit)
513	  assemble from the per-section \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) ones, the required
514	  naturality squares being the naturality of the sectionwise maps against the
515	  presheaf restriction morphisms. Packaging these as a
516	  \(\mathtt{Functor.CoreLaxMonoidal}\) yields the lax-monoidal structure; no
517	  new diagram chase is needed beyond transporting the sectionwise coherence.
518	  The composite \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) is then lax monoidal by
519	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\) together with the monoidality of
520	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) (Mathlib), modulo a defeq
521	  transport across the definitional unfolding of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\).
522	
523	  The commutativity hypothesis is essential: the presheaf-of-modules monoidal
524	  category requires \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-valued presheaves of rings, so the
525	  instance must be stated over \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-factored \(R, S\). In
526	  the project this holds: the relevant \(\varphi\) is
527	  \((\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\), which is
528	  structure-sheaf-valued and hence commutative-ring-valued.
529	\end{proof}
530	
531	\begin{lemma}
532	
533	\leanok
534	[Tensor product commutes with restriction along an open immersion]
535	  \label{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
536	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_restrict_iso}
537	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
538	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, let \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be
539	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, and let
540	  \(f : U \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion, with \((-)|_f\) the
541	  restriction of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along \(f\). The canonical comparison
542	  morphism
543	  \[
544	    (M \otimes_X N)\big|_f
545	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\;}\;
546	    M\big|_f \;\otimes_U\; N\big|_f
547	  \]
548	  is an isomorphism. No local-freeness, flatness, or line-bundle hypothesis on
549	  \(M, N\) is required: the statement holds for all \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
550	  The reason is structural to open immersions --- restriction along \(f\) is
551	  base change along the structure-sheaf \emph{isomorphism} induced by \(f\),
552	  not along a general ring map --- and base change along a ring isomorphism is
553	  trivially invertible and commutes with tensor products.
554	\end{lemma}
555	
556	\begin{proof}
557	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
558	  Write \(\varphi\) for the morphism of structure-sheaf-valued presheaves of
559	  rings induced by the open immersion \(f\) (concretely
560	  \(\varphi = (\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\)), so that
561	  restriction along \(f\) is computed by the inverse-image (pullback) functor on
562	  presheaves of modules. The proof exploits the defining feature of an open
563	  immersion: the comparison of structure-sheaf rings over the image is an
564	  \emph{isomorphism}, not merely a ring map. For an open \(V \subseteq U\) the
565	  section ring \(\Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr)\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) over the image
566	  of \(V\) is identified with \(\Gamma(U, V)\) by the canonical \emph{ring
567	  isomorphism}
568	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V : \Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr) \xrightarrow{\sim}
569	  \Gamma(U, V)\)
570	  (the \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) isomorphism
571	  \(\mathtt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.appIso}\)). Restriction along \(f\) is
572	  therefore extension of scalars along
573	  this ring isomorphism, and the proof needs no flatness anywhere.
574	
575	  \emph{Step 1 (reduce restriction to the abstract pullback).} The restriction
576	  \((-)|_f\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along the open immersion \(f\) is
577	  naturally isomorphic to the abstract presheaf-of-modules pullback
578	  \((\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(-)\), via the
579	  present comparison \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\). It
580	  therefore suffices to produce the comparison and prove it an isomorphism for
581	  the abstract pullback functor.
582	
583	  \emph{Step 2 (move the pullback inside the sheafification).} The substrate
584	  \(\otimes_X\) is sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
585	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}). The pullback commutes with
586	  sheafification: there is a natural isomorphism
587	  \(\mathtt{sheafification} \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
588	   \;\cong\;
589	   \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi \circ \mathtt{sheafification}\)
590	  (the present \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback}\)). Hence the
591	  whole question descends to the presheaf level, where it is the base-change
592	  comparison
593	  \[
594	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(A \otimes B)
595	      \;\longrightarrow\;
596	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A
597	      \;\otimes\;
598	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B
599	  \]
600	  for the underlying presheaves of modules \(A, B\) of \(M\) and \(N\).
601

603	  % \ref{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback} (the pair $(f^*, f_*)$ of
604	  % pullback/pushforward of $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a morphism of ringed
605	  % spaces is an adjoint pair)
606	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L252-273).

611	  % be a morphism of ringed spaces.
612	  % \begin{enumerate}
613	  % \item The functor
614	  % $f_* : \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \to \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_Y)$

616	  % \item The functor

618	  % is right exact. In fact it commutes with all colimits.
619	  %  [\dots]
620	  % \end{enumerate}
621	  % \end{lemma}

624	  % of functors, see
625	  % Sheaves, Lemma \ref{sheaves-lemma-adjoint-pullback-pushforward-modules}"
626	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
627	  \texttt{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback}: pullback and pushforward of
628	  $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a morphism form an adjoint pair $(f^*, f_*)$, the
629	  abstract universal property on which the comparison below rests.}

632	  presheaf level requires a sectionwise handle on the abstract functor
633	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\), which is defined as the left
634	  adjoint of pushforward (the presheaf-of-modules avatar of the
635	  \(f^* \dashv f_*\) adjunction of the cited Stacks lemma) and admits no
636	  sectionwise formula in Mathlib at the pinned commit. The closure passes through
637	  two presheaf-level ingredients, of which exactly one now remains open.
638	
639	  The first ingredient --- a strong-monoidal upgrade of
640	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along the structure-sheaf ring \emph{isomorphism}
641	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\), so that base change along the isomorphism commutes with
642	  \(\otimes\) --- is \emph{closed in this chapter} as
643	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
644	  (\(\mathtt{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}\)): for a ring isomorphism
645	  \(e : R \simeq S\) the canonical map \(a \otimes_R b \mapsto a \otimes_S b\) is an
646	  \(R\)-linear equivalence \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M) \otimes_R
647	  \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(N) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M
648	  \otimes_S N)\).

650	  The \emph{sole remaining residual} is therefore the presheaf-level identification
651	  \[
652	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \;\cong\; \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
653	  \]
654	  via \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\). One exhibits a
655	  presheaf-level adjunction
656	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) and concludes
657	  that \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\), being a left adjoint of
658	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\), is canonically isomorphic to the abstract left
659	  adjoint \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\). Here \(\beta\) is the structure map of the
660	  restriction functor, so that \((M|_f).\mathrm{val} =
661	  (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,M.\mathrm{val}\) definitionally;
662	  composing the resulting comparison with
663	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv} yields the asserted isomorphism.

666	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\), and must be built from
667	  presheaf-level \(\mathtt{pushforwardNatTrans}\) and

673	  existing sheaf-level template app-for-app at the presheaf level (a Mathlib-gradient
674	  build, not a deferral to an upstream PR).

676	  This lemma is the single substrate linchpin of the relative Picard group law for
677	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(M, N\): the associator
678	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} consumes the comparison
679	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_f \cong M|_f \otimes_U N|_f\) on its sole consumer
680	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}, where the restriction is commuted
681	  past the \emph{sheafified} tensor \(M \otimes_X N\) \emph{before} any local
682	  trivialisation of \(M, N\) is introduced --- so the comparison is required for the
683	  global, untrivialised modules, and no free-cover specialisation can stand in for it.
684	  Consequently the H1 residual \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong
685	  \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) is genuinely on the critical path, and closing it is

689	  presheaf-level adjunction itself bottom out; it is not the plan.

692	\begin{lemma}
693	
694	\leanok
695	[Base change along a ring isomorphism commutes with $\otimes$]
696	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
697	  \lean{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}
698	  Let \(e : R \simeq S\) be an isomorphism of commutative rings, and let \(M, N\) be
699	  \(S\)-modules. Restriction of scalars along \(e\) commutes with the tensor
700	  product: the canonical map
701	  \[
702	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M) \otimes_R \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(N)
703	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
704	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\bigl(M \otimes_S N\bigr),
705	    \qquad a \otimes_R b \;\longmapsto\; a \otimes_S b,
706	  \]
707	  is an \(R\)-linear equivalence. Equivalently, \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\) is
708	  \emph{strong} monoidal, not merely lax, when \(e\) is a ring \emph{isomorphism}:
709	  base change along an isomorphism \(R \simeq S\) identifies the two ground rings, so
710	  the lax structure map of \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\) is invertible. The
711	  forward map is \(R\)-linear, but its inverse \(a \otimes_S b \mapsto a \otimes_R b\)
712	  is only \emph{additive} (it is not \(S\)-linear), so the inverse is constructed as
713	  a homomorphism of additive groups and then verified to be the two-sided inverse of
714	  the forward \(R\)-linear map. This closes the strong-monoidal ingredient of
715	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, leaving the presheaf-level pushforward
716	  adjunction as the sole open residual there.
717	\end{lemma}

739	      (\(\mathtt{restrictScalars\_isIso\_\mu}\));

43	@[simps map_val, simps -isSimp obj_val]
44	noncomputable def pushforward : SheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} S where
45	  obj M :=
46	    { val := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ.hom).obj M.val
47	      isSheaf := ((F.sheafPushforwardContinuous _ J K).obj ⟨_, M.isSheaf⟩).property }
48	  map f :=
49	    { val := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ.hom).map f.val }

53	noncomputable abbrev over (M : SheafOfModules.{v} R) (X : D) : SheafOfModules.{v} (R.over X) :=

58	noncomputable abbrev Hom.over {M N : SheafOfModules.{v} R} (f : M ⟶ N) (X : D) :

103	      pushforward.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).map ψ) :=
104	  Iso.refl _
105	
106	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
107	lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_val_app (M U x) :
108	  ((pushforwardComp φ ψ).hom.app M).val.app U x = x := rfl
109	
110	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
111	lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_val_app (M U x) :
112	  ((pushforwardComp φ ψ).inv.app M).val.app U x = x := rfl
113	
114	variable {G' : D' ⥤ D''} {R'' : Sheaf K'' RingCat.{u}}
115	  [Functor.IsContinuous G' K' K'']
116	  [Functor.IsContinuous (G ⋙ G') K K'']
117	  [Functor.IsContinuous ((F ⋙ G) ⋙ G') J K'']
118	  [Functor.IsContinuous (F ⋙ G ⋙ G') J K'']
119	  (ψ' : R' ⟶ (G'.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K' K'').obj R'')
120	
121	lemma pushforward_assoc :
122	    (pushforward ψ').isoWhiskerLeft (pushforwardComp φ ψ) ≪≫
123	      pushforwardComp (F := F ⋙ G)
124	        (φ ≫ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).map ψ) ψ' =
125	    ((pushforward ψ').associator (pushforward ψ) (pushforward φ)).symm ≪≫
126	      isoWhiskerRight (pushforwardComp ψ ψ') (pushforward φ) ≪≫
127	      pushforwardComp (G := G ⋙ G') φ (ψ ≫
128	        (G.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K K').map ψ') := by ext; rfl

130	end

132	lemma pushforward_comp_id :
133	    pushforwardComp.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) φ =
134	      isoWhiskerLeft (pushforward.{v} φ) (pushforwardId S) ≪≫ rightUnitor _ := by ext; rfl
135	
136	lemma pushforward_id_comp :
137	    pushforwardComp.{v} (G := 𝟭 _) φ (𝟙 R) =
138	      isoWhiskerRight (pushforwardId R) (pushforward.{v} φ) ≪≫ leftUnitor _ := by ext; rfl

140	end

142	section NatTrans
143	
144	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
145	  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D}
146	  {F G H : C ⥤ D} {T : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {S : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
147	  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
148	  [Functor.IsContinuous G J K]
149	  [Functor.IsContinuous H J K]
150	  (φ : T ⟶ (G.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj S)
151	
152	/-- A natural transformation gives a natural transformation between the pushforward functors. -/
153	noncomputable
154	def pushforwardNatTrans (α : F ⟶ G) :
155	    pushforward.{v} φ ⟶
156	      pushforward.{v} (φ ≫ (Functor.sheafPushforwardContinuousNatTrans α _ _ _).app S) where
157	  app X :=
158	  { val.app U := (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.hom.app U).hom).map (X.val.map (α.app U.unop).op)
159	    val.naturality {U V} i := by
160	      ext x
161	      dsimp
162	      change (X.val.presheaf.map (G.map i.unop).op ≫ X.val.presheaf.map (α.app V.unop).op) _ =
163	        (X.val.presheaf.map (α.app U.unop).op ≫ X.val.presheaf.map (F.map i.unop).op) _
164	      simp only [← CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, α.naturality] }
165	  naturality {X Y} f := by
166	    ext U x
167	    exact congr($(f.val.naturality (α.app U.unop).op) x).symm
168	
169	@[simp] lemma pushforwardNatTrans_app_val_app (α : F ⟶ G) (M U x) :
170	    ((pushforwardNatTrans φ α).app M).val.app U x = M.val.map (α.app U.unop).op x := rfl
171	
172	@[simp]
173	lemma pushforwardNatTrans_id :
174	    pushforwardNatTrans φ (𝟙 G) = (pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)).hom := by cat_disch
175	
176	@[simp]
177	lemma pushforwardNatTrans_comp (α : F ⟶ G) (β : G ⟶ H)
178	    (φ : T ⟶ (H.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj S) :
179	    pushforwardNatTrans φ (α ≫ β) = pushforwardNatTrans φ β ≫ pushforwardNatTrans _ α ≫
180	      (pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)).hom := by cat_disch
181	
182	@[simp]
183	lemma pushforwardNatTrans_app_val_app_apply (α : F ⟶ G) (X U x) :
184	    ((pushforwardNatTrans φ α).app X).val.app U x = X.val.map (α.app U.unop).op x := rfl
185	
186	/-- A natural isomorphism gives a natural isomorphism between the pushforward functors. -/
187	@[simps]
188	noncomputable def pushforwardNatIso (α : F ≅ G) :
189	    pushforward.{v} φ ≅
190	      pushforward.{v} (φ ≫ (Functor.sheafPushforwardContinuousNatTrans α.hom _ _ _).app S) where
191	  hom := pushforwardNatTrans _ α.hom
192	  inv := pushforwardNatTrans _ α.inv ≫
193	    (pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp])).hom
194	  hom_inv_id := by
195	    ext X U x
196	    suffices X.val.presheaf.map (α.hom.app U.unop).op ≫
197	      X.val.presheaf.map (α.inv.app U.unop).op = 𝟙 _ from congr($this x)
198	    simp only [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp,
199	      Iso.inv_hom_id_app, op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
200	  inv_hom_id := by
201	    ext X U x
202	    suffices X.val.presheaf.map (α.inv.app U.unop).op ≫
203	      X.val.presheaf.map (α.hom.app U.unop).op = 𝟙 _ from congr($this x)
204	    simp only [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp,
205	      Iso.hom_inv_id_app, op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
206	
207	end NatTrans

209	section Adjunction
210	
211	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
212	  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {F : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C}
213	  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
214	  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
215	  [Functor.IsContinuous G K J]
216	  (adj : F ⊣ G)
217	  (φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)
218	  (ψ : R ⟶ (G.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K J).obj S)
219	  (H₁ : Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.counit) R.obj = ψ.hom ≫ G.op.whiskerLeft φ.hom)
220	  (H₂ : φ.hom ≫ F.op.whiskerLeft ψ.hom ≫
221	    Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.unit) S.obj = 𝟙 S.obj)
222	
223	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
224	/-- If `F ⊣ G`, then the pushforwards along `F` and `G` are also adjoint. -/
225	noncomputable
226	def pushforwardPushforwardAdj : pushforward.{v} φ ⊣ pushforward.{v} ψ where
227	  unit :=
228	    letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp G F K J K
229	    (pushforwardId _).inv ≫ pushforwardNatTrans (𝟙 _) adj.counit ≫
230	      (pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa)).hom ≫ (pushforwardComp _ _).inv
231	  counit :=
232	    letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp F G J K J
233	    (pushforwardComp _ _).hom ≫ pushforwardNatTrans _ adj.unit ≫
234	      (pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa)).hom ≫ (pushforwardId _).hom
235	  left_triangle_components X := by
236	    ext U x
237	    change (X.val.presheaf.map (adj.counit.app (F.obj U.unop)).op ≫
238	      X.val.presheaf.map (F.map (adj.unit.app U.unop)).op) _ = _
239	    dsimp only [id_obj]
240	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.left_triangle_components]
241	    simp
242	  right_triangle_components X := by
243	    ext U x
244	    change (X.val.presheaf.map (G.map (adj.counit.app U.unop)).op ≫
245	      X.val.presheaf.map (adj.unit.app (G.obj U.unop)).op) _ = _
246	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.right_triangle_components]
247	    simp
248	
249	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
250	lemma pushforwardPushforwardAdj_unit_app_val_app (M U x) :
251	    ((pushforwardPushforwardAdj adj φ ψ H₁ H₂).unit.app M).val.app U x =
252	      M.val.map (adj.counit.app U.unop).op x := rfl
253	
254	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
255	lemma pushforwardPushforwardAdj_counit_app_val_app (M U x) :
256	    ((pushforwardPushforwardAdj adj φ ψ H₁ H₂).counit.app M).val.app U x =
257	      M.val.map (adj.unit.app U.unop).op x := rfl
258	
259	noncomputable section
260	
261	open CategoryTheory Limits
262	
263	variable {C : Type u'} [Category.{v'} C] [HasBinaryProducts C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
264	  {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
265	
266	/-- The canonical morphism from `R` to the pushforward of its restriction to `Over x`. -/
267	def pushforwardOver (x : C) :
268	    R ⟶ ((Over.star x).sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat J (J.over x)).obj (R.over x) :=
269	  ⟨{app U := R.obj.map Limits.prod.snd.op
270	    naturality U V f := by simp [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp]; rfl }⟩
271	
272	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
273	/-- The adjunction between restriction to `Over x` and pushforward along `Over.star x`. -/
274	def overPushforwardOverAdj (x : C) :
275	    pushforward.{w} (𝟙 (R.over x)) ⊣ pushforward.{w} (pushforwardOver x) := by
276	  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (Over.forgetAdjStar x) (𝟙 (R.over x)) _ ?_ ?_
277	  · ext y : 2
278	    simp [pushforwardOver]
279	  · ext y : 2
280	    simp [pushforwardOver, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp]
281	
282	instance (x : C) : IsLeftAdjoint (pushforward.{w} (𝟙 (R.over x))) where
283	  exists_rightAdjoint := ⟨_, Nonempty.intro (overPushforwardOverAdj x)⟩
284	
285	end
286	
287	end Adjunction

289	section Equivalence
290	
291	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
292	  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} (eqv : C ≌ D)
293	  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
294	  [Functor.IsContinuous eqv.functor J K]
295	  [Functor.IsContinuous eqv.inverse K J]
296	  (φ : S ⟶ (eqv.functor.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)
297	  (ψ : R ⟶ (eqv.inverse.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} K J).obj S)
298	  (H₁ : Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.counit) R.obj =
299	    ψ.hom ≫ eqv.inverse.op.whiskerLeft φ.hom)
300	  (H₂ : φ.hom ≫ eqv.functor.op.whiskerLeft ψ.hom ≫
301	    Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.unit) S.obj = 𝟙 S.obj)
302	
303	/-- If `e : C ≌ D`, then the pushforwards along `e.functor` and `e.inverse` forms an equivalence. -/
304	noncomputable
305	def pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence : SheafOfModules R ≌ SheafOfModules S where
306	  functor := pushforward.{v} φ
307	  inverse := pushforward.{v} ψ
308	  unitIso :=
309	    letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp eqv.inverse eqv.functor K J K
310	    (pushforwardId _).symm ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ eqv.counitIso ≪≫
311	      pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa) ≪≫ (pushforwardComp _ _).symm
312	  counitIso :=
313	    letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp eqv.functor eqv.inverse J K J
314	    pushforwardComp _ _ ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ eqv.unitIso ≪≫
315	      pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa) ≪≫ pushforwardId _
316	  functor_unitIso_comp :=
317	    (pushforwardPushforwardAdj eqv.toAdjunction φ ψ H₁ H₂).left_triangle_components
318	
319	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
320	lemma pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_unit_app_val_app (M U x) :
321	    ((pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence eqv φ ψ H₁ H₂).unit.app M).val.app U x =
322	      M.val.map (eqv.counit.app U.unop).op x := rfl
323	
324	-- Not a simp because the type of the LHS is dsimp-able
325	lemma pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_counit_app_val_app (M U x) :
326	    ((pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence eqv φ ψ H₁ H₂).counit.app M).val.app U x =
327	      M.val.map (eqv.unit.app U.unop).op x := rfl
328	
329	end Equivalence

331	end SheafOfModules

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.ChangeOfRings
9	
10	/-!
11	# Pushforward of presheaves of modules
12	
13	If `F : C ⥤ D`, the precomposition `F.op ⋙ _` induces a functor from presheaves
14	over `D` to presheaves over `C`. When `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat`, we define the
15	induced functor `pushforward₀ : PresheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} (F.op ⋙ R)`
16	on presheaves of modules.
17	
18	In case we have a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, we also construct
19	a functor `pushforward : PresheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} S`, and
20	we show that they interact with the composition of morphisms similarly as pseudofunctors.
21	
22	-/
23	
24	@[expose] public section
25	
26	universe v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u
27	
28	open CategoryTheory Functor
29	
30	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
31	  {E : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} E] {E' : Type u₄} [Category.{v₄} E']
32	
33	namespace PresheafOfModules
34	
35	variable (F : C ⥤ D)
36	
37	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
38	/-- Implementation of `pushforward₀`. -/
39	@[simps]
40	def pushforward₀_obj (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) (M : PresheafOfModules R) :
41	    PresheafOfModules (F.op ⋙ R) :=
42	  { obj X := ModuleCat.of _ (M.obj (F.op.obj X))
43	    map {X Y} f := M.map (F.op.map f)
44	    map_id X := by
45	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext
46	        -- Work around an instance diamond for `restrictScalarsId'`
47	        (@LinearMap.ext _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (_) (_) _ _ _ (fun x => ?_))
48	      exact (M.congr_map_apply (F.op.map_id X) x).trans (by simp)
49	    map_comp := fun f g ↦ by
50	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext
51	        -- Work around an instance diamond for `restrictScalarsId'`
52	        (@LinearMap.ext _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (_) (_) _ _ _ (fun x => ?_))
53	      exact (M.congr_map_apply (F.op.map_comp f g) x).trans (by simp) }
54	
55	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
56	/-- The pushforward functor on presheaves of modules for a functor `F : C ⥤ D` and
57	`R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat`. On the underlying presheaves of abelian groups, it is induced
58	by the precomposition with `F.op`. -/
59	def pushforward₀ (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
60	    PresheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} (F.op ⋙ R) where
61	  obj M := pushforward₀_obj F R M
62	  map {M₁ M₂} φ := { app X := φ.app _ }
63	
64	/-- If `F : C ⥤ D` if a functor and `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat` is a presheaf
65	of commutative rings, this is the pushforward functor from the category
66	of presheaves of modules on `R` to the category of presheaves of
67	modules on `F.op ⋙ R`. -/
68	abbrev pushforward₀OfCommRingCat (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}) :
69	    PresheafOfModules.{v} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _) ⥤
70	      PresheafOfModules.{v} ((F.op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ _ _) :=
71	  pushforward₀ F (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)
72	
73	/-- The pushforward of presheaves of modules commutes with the forgetful functor
74	to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
75	noncomputable def pushforward₀CompToPresheaf (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
76	    pushforward₀.{v} F R ⋙ toPresheaf _ ≅ toPresheaf _ ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op :=
77	  Iso.refl _
78	
79	variable {F}
80	variable {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
81	
82	attribute [local simp] pushforward₀ in
83	/-- The pushforward functor `PresheafOfModules R ⥤ PresheafOfModules S` induced by
84	a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`. -/
85	@[simps! obj_obj]
86	noncomputable def pushforward : PresheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} S :=
87	  pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ
88	
89	/-- The pushforward of presheaves of modules commutes with the forgetful functor
90	to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
91	noncomputable def pushforwardCompToPresheaf :
92	    pushforward.{v} φ ⋙ toPresheaf _ ≅ toPresheaf _ ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op :=
93	  Iso.refl _
94	
95	lemma pushforward_obj_map_apply (M : PresheafOfModules.{v} R) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
96	    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
97	      (((pushforward φ).obj M).map f).hom m = M.map (F.map f.unop).op m := rfl
98	
99	/-- `@[simp]`-normal form of `pushforward_obj_map_apply`. -/
100	@[simp]
101	lemma pushforward_obj_map_apply' (M : PresheafOfModules.{v} R) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
102	    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
103	      DFunLike.coe
104	        (F := ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _) →ₗ[_]
105	          ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars (S.map f).hom).obj ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _)))
106	        (((pushforward φ).obj M).map f).hom m = M.map (F.map f.unop).op m := rfl
107	
108	lemma pushforward_map_app_apply {M N : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} (α : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
109	    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
110	    (((pushforward φ).map α).app X).hom m = α.app (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)) m := rfl
111	
112	/-- `@[simp]`-normal form of `pushforward_map_app_apply`. -/
113	@[simp]
114	lemma pushforward_map_app_apply' {M N : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} (α : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
115	    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
116	    DFunLike.coe
117	      (F := ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _) →ₗ[_] ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _))
118	      (((pushforward φ).map α).app X).hom m = α.app (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)) m := rfl
119

121	
122	variable (R) in
123	/-- The pushforward functor by the identity morphism identifies to
124	the identify functor of the category of presheaves of modules. -/
125	noncomputable def pushforwardId :
126	    pushforward.{v} (S := R) (F := 𝟭 _) (𝟙 R) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
127	  Iso.refl _
128	
129	section
130	
131	variable {T : Eᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {G : D ⥤ E} (ψ : R ⟶ G.op ⋙ T)
132	
133	/-- The composition of two pushforward functors on categories of presheaves of modules
134	identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
135	noncomputable def pushforwardComp :
136	    pushforward.{v} ψ ⋙ pushforward.{v} φ ≅
137	      pushforward.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ) :=
138	  Iso.refl _
139	
140	variable {T' : E'ᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {G' : E ⥤ E'} (ψ' : T ⟶ G'.op ⋙ T')
141	
142	lemma pushforward_assoc :
143	    (pushforward ψ').isoWhiskerLeft (pushforwardComp φ ψ) ≪≫
144	      pushforwardComp (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ F.op.whiskerLeft ψ) ψ' =
145	    ((pushforward ψ').associator (pushforward ψ) (pushforward φ)).symm ≪≫
146	      isoWhiskerRight (pushforwardComp ψ ψ') (pushforward φ) ≪≫
147	        pushforwardComp (G := G ⋙ G') φ (ψ ≫ G.op.whiskerLeft ψ') := by ext; rfl
148	
149	end
150	
151	lemma pushforward_comp_id :
152	    pushforwardComp.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) φ =
153	      isoWhiskerLeft (pushforward.{v} φ) (pushforwardId S) ≪≫ rightUnitor _ := by ext; rfl
154	
155	lemma pushforward_id_comp :
156	    pushforwardComp.{v} (G := 𝟭 _) φ (𝟙 R) =
157	      isoWhiskerRight (pushforwardId R) (pushforward.{v} φ) ≪≫ leftUnitor _ := by ext; rfl
158	
159	end
160	
161	end PresheafOfModules

13	/-!
14	# Pullback of presheaves of modules
15	
16	Let `F : C ⥤ D` be a functor, `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` and `S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` be presheaves
17	of rings, and `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` be a morphism of presheaves of rings,
18	we introduce the pullback functor `pullback : PresheafOfModules S ⥤ PresheafOfModules R`
19	as the left adjoint of `pushforward : PresheafOfModules R ⥤ PresheafOfModules S`.
20	The existence of this left adjoint functor is obtained under suitable universe assumptions.
21	
22	From the compatibility of `pushforward` with respect to composition, we deduce
23	similar pseudofunctor-like properties of the `pullback` functors.
24	
25	-/
26	
27	@[expose] public section
28	
29	universe v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite Functor
32	
33	namespace PresheafOfModules
34	
35	section
36	
37	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
38	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
39	  [(pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint]
40	
41	/-- The pullback functor `PresheafOfModules S ⥤ PresheafOfModules R` induced by
42	a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, defined as the left adjoint
43	functor to the pushforward, when it exists. -/
44	noncomputable def pullback : PresheafOfModules.{v} S ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} R :=
45	  (pushforward.{v} φ).leftAdjoint
46	
47	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, this is the adjunction
48	between associated pullback and pushforward functors on the categories
49	of presheaves of modules. -/
50	noncomputable def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback.{v} φ ⊣ pushforward.{v} φ :=
51	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (pushforward φ)
52	
53	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, this is the property
54	that the (partial) left adjoint functor of `pushforward φ` is defined
55	on a certain object `M : PresheafOfModules S`. -/
56	abbrev pullbackObjIsDefined : ObjectProperty (PresheafOfModules.{v} S) :=
57	  (pushforward φ).leftAdjointObjIsDefined
58	
59	end
60	
61	section
62	
63	variable {C D : Type u} [SmallCategory C] [SmallCategory D]
64	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
65	
66	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, where `F : C ⥤ D`,
67	`S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat`, `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` and `X : C`, the (partial) left adjoint
68	functor of `pushforward φ` is defined on the object `(free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)`:
69	this object shall be mapped to `(free R).obj (yoneda.obj (F.obj X))`. -/
70	noncomputable def pushforwardCompCoyonedaFreeYonedaCorepresentableBy (X : C) :
71	    (pushforward φ ⋙ coyoneda.obj (op ((free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)))).CorepresentableBy
72	      ((free R).obj (yoneda.obj (F.obj X))) where
73	  homEquiv {M} := freeYonedaEquiv.trans
74	    (freeYonedaEquiv (M := (pushforward φ).obj M)).symm
75	  homEquiv_comp {M N} g f := freeYonedaEquiv.injective (by
76	    dsimp
77	    erw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply, freeYonedaEquiv_comp]
78	    conv_rhs => erw [freeYonedaEquiv_comp]
79	    erw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
80	    rfl)
81	
82	lemma pullbackObjIsDefined_free_yoneda (X : C) :
83	    pullbackObjIsDefined φ ((free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)) :=
84	  (pushforwardCompCoyonedaFreeYonedaCorepresentableBy φ X).isCorepresentable
85	
86	lemma pullbackObjIsDefined_eq_top :
87	    pullbackObjIsDefined.{u} φ = ⊤ := by
88	  ext M
89	  simp only [Pi.top_apply, Prop.top_eq_true, iff_true]
90	  apply leftAdjointObjIsDefined_of_isColimit
91	    M.isColimitFreeYonedaCoproductsCokernelCofork
92	  rintro (_ | _)
93	  all_goals
94	    apply leftAdjointObjIsDefined_colimit _
95	      (fun _ ↦ pullbackObjIsDefined_free_yoneda _ _)
96	
97	instance : (pushforward.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
98	  isRightAdjoint_of_leftAdjointObjIsDefined_eq_top
99	    (pullbackObjIsDefined_eq_top φ)
100	
101	end
102	
103	section
104	
105	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
106	  {E : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} E] {E' : Type u₄} [Category.{v₄} E']
107	
108	variable {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
109	  {G : D ⥤ E} {T : Eᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (ψ : R ⟶ G.op ⋙ T)
110	
111	instance : (pushforward.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S)).IsRightAdjoint :=
112	  isRightAdjoint_of_iso (pushforwardId.{v} S).symm
113	
114	variable (S) in
115	/-- The pullback by the identity morphism identifies to the identity functor of the
116	category of presheaves of modules. -/
117	noncomputable def pullbackId : pullback.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
118	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S))).leftAdjointIdIso (pushforwardId S)
119	
120	variable [(pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint]
121	
122	section
123	
124	variable [(pushforward.{v} ψ).IsRightAdjoint]
125	
126	instance : (pushforward.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ)).IsRightAdjoint :=
127	  isRightAdjoint_of_iso (pushforwardComp.{v} φ ψ)
128	
129	/-- The composition of two pullback functors on presheaves of modules identifies
130	to the pullback for the composition. -/
131	noncomputable def pullbackComp :
132	    pullback.{v} φ ⋙ pullback.{v} ψ ≅
133	      pullback.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ) :=
134	  Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso
135	    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} φ) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} ψ)
136	    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ))
137	    (pushforwardComp φ ψ)
138	
139	variable {T' : E'ᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {G' : E ⥤ E'} (ψ' : T ⟶ G'.op ⋙ T')
140	  [(pushforward.{v} ψ').IsRightAdjoint]
141	
142	lemma pullback_assoc :
143	    isoWhiskerLeft _ (pullbackComp.{v} ψ ψ') ≪≫
144	      pullbackComp.{v} (G := G ⋙ G') φ (ψ ≫ whiskerLeft G.op ψ') =
145	    (associator _ _ _).symm ≪≫ isoWhiskerRight (pullbackComp.{v} φ ψ) _ ≪≫
146	        pullbackComp.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ) ψ' :=
147	  Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso_assoc _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (pushforward_assoc φ ψ ψ')
148	
149	end
150	
151	lemma pullback_id_comp :
152	    pullbackComp.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) φ =
153	      isoWhiskerRight (pullbackId S) (pullback φ) ≪≫ Functor.leftUnitor _ :=
154	  Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso_id_comp _ _ _ _ (pushforward_comp_id φ)
155	
156	lemma pullback_comp_id :
157	    pullbackComp.{v} (G := 𝟭 _) φ (𝟙 R) =
158	      isoWhiskerLeft _ (pullbackId R) ≪≫ Functor.rightUnitor _ :=
159	  Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso_comp_id _ _ _ _ (pushforward_id_comp φ)
160	
161	end
162	
163	end PresheafOfModules

17	/-!
18	# The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme
19	
20	In this file, we define the abelian category of sheaves of modules
21	`X.Modules` over a scheme `X`, and study its basic functoriality.
22	
23	-/
24	
25	@[expose] public section
26	
27	universe t u
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace SheafOfModules Bicategory
30	
31	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
32	
33	variable {X Y Z T : Scheme.{u}}
34	
35	variable (X) in
36	/-- The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme. -/
37	def Modules := SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf
38	
39	namespace Modules
40	
41	/-- Morphisms between `𝒪ₓ`-modules. Use `Hom.app` to act on sections. -/
42	def Hom (M N : X.Modules) : Type u := SheafOfModules.Hom M N
43	
44	instance : Category X.Modules where
45	  Hom := Modules.Hom
46	  __ := (inferInstance : Category (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf))
47	
48	noncomputable instance : Abelian X.Modules :=
49	  inferInstanceAs <| Abelian (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf)
50	instance : HasLimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasLimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
51	instance : HasColimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasColimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
52	
53	section Functor
54	
55	variable (X) in
56	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules.
57	This is mostly useful to transport results from (pre)sheaves of modules to `𝒪ₓ`-modules and
58	usually shouldn't be used directly when working with actual `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
59	def toPresheafOfModules : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules := SheafOfModules.forget _
60	
61	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules is fully faithful. -/
62	def fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules : (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).FullyFaithful :=
63	  SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _
64	
65	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Full := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.full
66	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Faithful := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.faithful
67	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).IsRightAdjoint :=
68	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isRightAdjoint
69	
70	variable (X) in
71	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
72	noncomputable def toPresheaf : X.Modules ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf Ab X :=
73	  toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _
74	
75	instance : (toPresheaf X).Faithful := .comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
76	instance : PreservesLimits (toPresheaf X) := comp_preservesLimits _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
77	instance : (toPresheaf X).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
78	  reflectsIsomorphisms_comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
79	
80	end Functor
81	
82	variable {M N K : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} {U V : X.Opens}
83	
84	section Presheaf
85	
86	/-- The underlying abelian presheaf of an `𝒪ₓ`-module. -/
87	noncomputable def presheaf (M : X.Modules) : TopCat.Presheaf Ab X := M.1.presheaf
88	
89	/-- Notation for sections of a presheaf of module. -/
90	scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation3 "Γ(" M ", " U ")" => (Scheme.Modules.presheaf M).obj (.op U)
91	
92	instance : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, U) := (M.val.obj (.op U)).isModule
93	
94	variable (M) in
95	@[simp] lemma map_smul (i : U ⟶ V) (r : Γ(X, V)) (x : Γ(M, V)) :
96	    M.presheaf.map i.op (r • x) = X.presheaf.map i.op r • M.presheaf.map i.op x :=
97	  M.val.map_smul _ _ _
98	
99	/-- The underlying map between abelian presheaves of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
100	noncomputable def Hom.mapPresheaf (φ : M ⟶ N) : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf :=
101	  (toPresheaf X).map φ
102	
103	/-- The application of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules to sections. -/
104	def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=
105	  (forget₂ _ _).map (φ.val.app (.op U))
106	
107	@[simp] lemma mapPresheaf_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U) : φ.mapPresheaf.app U = φ.app U.unop := rfl
108	
109	@[simp]
110	lemma Hom.app_smul (φ : M ⟶ N) (r : Γ(X, U)) (x : Γ(M, U)) :
111	    φ.app U (r • x) = r • φ.app U x :=
112	  (φ.val.app (.op U)).hom.map_smul r x
113	
114	@[simp] lemma Hom.add_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ + ψ).app U = φ.app U + ψ.app U := rfl
115	@[simp] lemma Hom.sub_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ - ψ).app U = φ.app U - ψ.app U := rfl
116	@[simp] lemma Hom.zero_app : (0 : M ⟶ N).app U = 0 := rfl
117	@[simp] lemma Hom.id_app (M : X.Modules) : (𝟙 M :).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
118	@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl
119	
120	@[ext]
121	lemma hom_ext (f g : M ⟶ N) (H : ∀ U, f.app U = g.app U) : f = g := by
122	  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
123	  ext U x
124	  exact congr($(H U.unop) x)
125	
126	lemma isSheaf (M : X.Modules) : M.presheaf.IsSheaf := SheafOfModules.isSheaf M
127	
128	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_obj : (toPresheaf X).obj M = M.presheaf := rfl
129	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map : (toPresheaf X).map φ = φ.mapPresheaf := rfl
130	
131	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
132	lemma Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app {M N : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} :
133	    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U) := by
134	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (toPresheaf X), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
135	  simp [Opposite.op_surjective.forall]
136	
137	instance [IsIso φ] : IsIso (φ.app U) := Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app.mp ‹_› _
138	
139	@[simp, push ←]
140	lemma inv_app [IsIso φ] : (inv φ).app U = inv (φ.app U) := by
141	  apply IsIso.eq_inv_of_hom_inv_id
142	  simp [← Hom.comp_app]
143	
144	end Presheaf

/-- Restrictions scalars along equal ring homomorphisms are naturally isomorphic. -/
def restrictScalarsCongr
    {R : Type u₁} {S : Type u₂} [Ring R] [Ring S] {f g : R →+* S} (e : f = g) :
    ModuleCat.restrictScalars f ≅ ModuleCat.restrictScalars g :=
  NatIso.ofComponents (fun X ↦ LinearEquiv.toModuleIso
    (X₁ := (ModuleCat.restrictScalars f).obj X) (X₂ := (ModuleCat.restrictScalars g).obj X)
    { __ := AddEquiv.refl _, map_smul' _ _ := by subst e; rfl }) fun _ ↦ by subst e; rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictScalarsCongr_symm
    {R : Type u₁} {S : Type u₂} [Ring R] [Ring S] {f g : R →+* S} (e : f = g) :
  (restrictScalarsCongr e).symm = restrictScalarsCongr e.symm := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictScalarsCongr_hom_app
    {R : Type u₁} {S : Type u₂} [Ring R] [Ring S] {f g : R →+* S} (e : f = g)
    (M : ModuleCat S) (x : M) :
  (restrictScalarsCongr e).hom.app M x = x := rfl

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the presheaf-level pushforward adjunction (H1)

De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.{pushforwardNatTrans, pushforwardCongr,
pushforwardPushforwardAdj}`
(`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean`, L154/L73/L226) to the
`PresheafOfModules` level. Every line of the sheaf template already manipulates `.val`/`.val.presheaf`
presheaf data, so the de-sheafification is mechanical (drop the `Sheaf` wrapper and the sheaf-only
`IsContinuous` `letI`s). These are the **H1** linchpin of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`: from a pair
`adj : F ⊣ G` one obtains a presheaf-level adjunction `pushforward φ ⊣ pushforward ψ`, hence — against
the existing `PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` and via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq` —
the iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ` that moves the abstract presheaf pullback onto the concrete
restriction pushforward. Per blueprint `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, Step 3 (the H1 residual). -/

namespace PresheafOfModules

open CategoryTheory Functor

section PushforwardNatTrans

universe v₁ v₂ uC uD

variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
  {F G : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
  (φ : S ⟶ G.op ⋙ R)

/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardNatTrans`.** A natural transformation `α : F ⟶ G` of functors
`C ⥤ D` induces a natural transformation between the pushforward functors along `F` and `G`.
De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans`
(`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:154`), dropping the `.val` wrapper. -/
noncomputable def pushforwardNatTrans (α : F ⟶ G) :
    pushforward.{u} φ ⟶ pushforward.{u} (φ ≫ Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op α) R) where
  app M :=
    { app := fun U => (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app U).hom).map (M.map (α.app U.unop).op)
      naturality := fun {U V} i => by
        ext x
        dsimp
        change (M.presheaf.map (G.map i.unop).op ≫ M.presheaf.map (α.app V.unop).op) _ =
          (M.presheaf.map (α.app U.unop).op ≫ M.presheaf.map (F.map i.unop).op) _
        simp only [← CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, α.naturality] }
  naturality := fun {M N} f => by
    ext U x
    exact congr($(f.naturality (α.app U.unop).op) x).symm

@[simp] lemma pushforwardNatTrans_app_app_apply (α : F ⟶ G) (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R)
    (U : Cᵒᵖ) (x) :
    ((pushforwardNatTrans φ α).app M).app U x = M.map (α.app U.unop).op x := rfl

end PushforwardNatTrans

section PushforwardCongr

universe v₁ v₂ uC uD

variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
  {F : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}

/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardCongr`.** Pushforwards along equal morphisms of presheaves of
rings are isomorphic. De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr`
(`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:73`), dropping the `fullyFaithfulForget` preimage (the
presheaf-level `pushforward` lands in `PresheafOfModules` directly). -/
noncomputable def pushforwardCongr {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ) :
    pushforward.{u} φ ≅ pushforward.{u} ψ :=
  NatIso.ofComponents (fun M ↦
    PresheafOfModules.isoMk
      (fun U ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr (R := S.obj U) (S := R.obj _)
        (f := (φ.app U).hom) (g := (ψ.app U).hom) (by subst e; rfl)).app _)
      (fun _ _ _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)) (fun _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ)
    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R) (U x) :
    ((pushforwardCongr e).hom.app M).app U x = x := by subst e; rfl

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ)
    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R) (U x) :
    ((pushforwardCongr e).inv.app M).app U x = x := by subst e; rfl

end PushforwardCongr

section PushforwardAdj

universe v₁ v₂ uC uD

variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
  {F : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
  (adj : F ⊣ G)
  (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
  (ψ : R ⟶ G.op ⋙ S)
  (H₁ : Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.counit) R = ψ ≫ G.op.whiskerLeft φ)
  (H₂ : φ ≫ F.op.whiskerLeft ψ ≫ Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.unit) S = 𝟙 S)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardPushforwardAdj`.** If `F ⊣ G`, then the presheaf-of-modules
pushforwards along `F` and `G` are also adjoint. De-sheafification of
`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:226`), dropping the
sheaf-only `IsContinuous` `letI`s and the `.val`/`.hom` wrappers. This is the H1 linchpin: applied
to the open-immersion adjunction `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base` it gives
`pushforward β ⊣ pushforward φ`. -/
noncomputable def pushforwardPushforwardAdj : pushforward.{u} φ ⊣ pushforward.{u} ψ where
  unit :=
    (pushforwardId _).inv ≫ pushforwardNatTrans (𝟙 _) adj.counit ≫
      (pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa using H₁)).hom ≫ (pushforwardComp _ _).inv
  counit :=
    (pushforwardComp _ _).hom ≫ pushforwardNatTrans _ adj.unit ≫
      (pushforwardCongr (by ext1; simpa using H₂)).hom ≫ (pushforwardId _).hom
  left_triangle_components X := by
    ext U x
    change (X.presheaf.map (adj.counit.app (F.obj U.unop)).op ≫
      X.presheaf.map (F.map (adj.unit.app U.unop)).op) _ = _
    dsimp only [id_obj]
    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.left_triangle_components]
    simp
  right_triangle_components X := by
    ext U x
    change (X.presheaf.map (G.map (adj.counit.app U.unop)).op ≫
      X.presheaf.map (adj.unit.app (G.obj U.unop)).op) _ = _
    rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, adj.right_triangle_components]
    simp

end PushforwardAdj

end PresheafOfModules
